devanagari: विदुर उवाच । ये त्वयाभिहिता ब्रह्मन् सुताः प्राचीनबर्हिषः । ते रुद्रगीतेन हरिं सिद्धिमापुः प्रतोष्य काम् ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: vidura uvāca ye tvayābhihitā brahman sutāḥ prācīnabarhiṣaḥ te rudragītena hariṃ siddhimāpuḥ pratoṣya kām

translation:
Vidura said:
What well-being did the sons of Prācīnabarhis mentioned by you, accomplish by propitiating Hari through repeatedly reciting the hymn sung by Rudra, Oh Brahman.

devanagari: किं बार्हस्पत्येह परत्र वाथ । कैवल्यनाथप्रियपार्श्ववर्तिनः । आसाद्य देवं गिरिशं यदृच्छया । प्रापुः परं नूनमथ प्रचेतसः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: kiṃ bārhaspatyeha paratra vātha kaivalyanāthapriyapārśvavartinaḥ āsādya devaṃ giriśaṃ yadṛcchayā prāpuḥ paraṃ nūnamatha pracetasaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active third plural], [aorist active third plural]

translation: Oh pupil of Bṛhaspati (Maitreya)! Meeting by a lucky chance god Śiva (the Lord of mount Kailāsa), the Pracetasas who won the grace (lit. became favourite personal attendants of the Lord of Kaivalya or Liberation.[1] must have certainly attained to their highest goal in life (viz. Liberation or Mokṣa). Please tell me: Have they attained it here or hereafter?

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । प्रचेतसोऽन्तरुदधौ पितुरादेशकारिणः । अपयज्ञेन तपसा पुरञ्जनणम् अतोषयन् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvāca pracetaso apayajñena tapasā purañjanaṇam atoṣayan

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Maitreya said:
Pracetasas who wished to carry out the order of their father remained under the waters of the sea (-like lake)[2] and propitiated the creator of all bodies (i e. Viṣṇu) by worshipping him with repeated recitation (of the hymn sung by Rudra) and by practising austerities.

devanagari: दशवर्षसहस्रान्ते पुरुषस्तु सनातनः । तेषां आविरभूत् कृच्छ्रं शान्तेन शमयन् रुचा ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: daśavarṣasahasrānte puruṣastu sanātanaḥ teṣāṃ āvirabhūt kṛcchraṃ śāntena śamayan rucā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [instrumental single from √ śam class 4 verb], [instrumental single from √ śam class 9 verb]
[instrumental single from √ śam class 4 verb], [instrumental single from √ śam class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ śam ], [vocative single from √ śam ]

translation: At the end of (the penance covering) ten thousand years, the Eternal Supreme Man (Lord Viṣṇu) manifested himself to them in a purely sāttvika form, completely pacifying their trouble of penance by his soothing splendour.

devanagari: सुपर्णस्कन्धमारूढो मेरुश्रूङ्गमिवाम्बुदः । पीतवासा मणिग्रीवः कुर्वन् वितिमिरा दिशः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: suparṇaskandhamārūḍho meruśrūṅgamivāmbudaḥ pītavāsā maṇigrīvaḥ kurvan vitimirā diśaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: Like a (dark-blue) cloud on the peak of mount Meru (which is of pure gold) he rode over the shoulders of Garuḍa. He wore a yellow garment and had the Kaustubha gem in his necklace. By his splendour he dispelled the darkness from all directions.

devanagari: काशिष्णुना कनकवर्णविभूषणेन । भ्राजत्कपोलवदनो विलसत्किरीटः । अष्टायुधैरनुचरैर्मुनिभिः सुरेन्द्रैः । आसेवितो गरुडकिन्नरगीतकीर्तिः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: kāśiṣṇunā kanakavarṇavibhūṣaṇena bhrājatkapolavadano vilasatkirīṭaḥ aṣṭāyudhairanucarairmunibhiḥ surendraiḥ āsevito garuḍakinnaragītakīrtiḥ
translation: His cheeks as well as his entire face appeared refulgent with bright gold ornaments (shedding a spectrum of variegated light of the precious stones set in ornaments). He wore a brilliant crown. He was wielding (lit. was attended upon by) eight weapons[3] (in his eight hands). He was waited upon by his attendants, sages and prominent gods (like Brahmā); His glory was being sung by the celestial singer Garuḍa with the fluttering of his (Veda-) wings.
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā enumerates them: a conch, the disc—Sudarśana, the mace Kaumodakī, a lotus, the Śārṅga bow, an arrow, a sword, a shield. He defends the inclusion of a lotus as weapon by chatri-nyāya.

devanagari: पीनायताष्टभुजमण्डल मध्यलक्ष्म्या । स्पर्धच्छ्रिया परिवृतो वनमालयाऽद्यः । बर्हिष्मतः पुरुष आह सुतान् प्रपन्नान् । पर्जन्यनादरुतया सघृणावल...
english_devnagari: pīnāyatāṣṭabhujamaṇḍala madhyalakṣmyā spardhacchriyā parivṛto vanamālayā barhiṣmataḥ puruṣa āha sutān prapannān parjanyanādarutayā saghṛṇāvalokaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ spṛdh class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ spṛdh class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ spṛdh class 1 verb]
[instrumental single from √ ru class 1 verb], [instrumental single from √ ru class 2 verb]

translation: The First (Ancient-most or Supreme) Man was garlanded with a wreath of forest-flowers (vanamālā) which vied in beauty with the goddess Lakṣmī who beautified the central space (His chest) encircled by His eight long and mighty arms. He looked with compassion at the sons of Barhiṣmat (the Pracetasas) who sought his protection, and addressed to them in deep voice like the rumbling of clouds.

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । वरं वृणीध्वं भद्रं वो यूयं मे नृपनन्दनाः । सौहार्देनापृथग्धर्माः तुष्टोऽहं सौहृदेन वः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca varaṃ vṛṇīdhvaṃ bhadraṃ vo yūyaṃ me nṛpanandanāḥ sauhārdenāpṛthagdharmāḥ tuṣṭo

translation:
The Venerable Lord said:
May you be blessed, Oh Princes! Due to your strong mutual love, you are following the same religious observances. I am highly pleased with you by your brotherly affection. You seek a boon from me.

devanagari: योऽनुस्मरति सन्ध्यायां युष्मान् अनुदिनं नरः । तस्य भ्रातृष्वात्मसाम्यं तथा भूतेषु सौहृदम् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: yo tasya bhrātṛṣvātmasāmyaṃ tathā bhūteṣu sauhṛdam
translation: A man who will daily remember you at dusk and dawn, will love his brothers like his own self and will cherish friendliness to all living beings.

devanagari: ये तु मां रुद्रगीतेन सायं प्रातः समाहिताः । स्तुवन्त्यहं कामवरान् दास्ये प्रज्ञां च शोभनाम् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: ye tu māṃ rudragītena sāyaṃ prātaḥ samāhitāḥ stuvantyahaṃ kāmavarān dāsye prajñāṃ ca śobhanām

translation: I shall bestow whatever boon is desired from me and brilliant intellect as well, to those who every morning and evening praise me with concentrated mind, with the hymn (which Rudra sang).

devanagari: यद्यूयं पितुरादेशं अग्रहीष्ट मुदान्विताः । अथो व उशती कीर्तिः लोकाननु भविष्यति ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: yadyūyaṃ piturādeśaṃ agrahīṣṭa mudānvitāḥ atho va uśatī kīrtiḥ lokānanu bhaviṣyati

[nominative dual from √ vaś class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ vaś class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ vaś class 2 verb]

translation: Since you have, with gladness, accepted and carried out the command of your father, your enviable glory will spread all over the worlds.

devanagari: भविता विश्रुतः पुत्रो ऽनवमो ब्रह्मणो गुणैः । य एतां आत्मवीर्येण त्रिलोकीं पूरयिष्यति ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: bhavitā viśrutaḥ putro ya etāṃ ātmavīryeṇa trilokīṃ pūrayiṣyati
translation: You will have a glorious son who is not inferior to Brahmā (the creator) in excellent attributes and who will fill (i.e. populate) the whole of these three worlds with his progeny.

devanagari: कण्डोः प्रम्लोचया लब्धा कन्या कमललोचना । तां चापविद्धां जगृहुः भूरुहा नृपनन्दनाः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: kaṇḍoḥ pramlocayā labdhā kanyā kamalalocanā tāṃ cāpaviddhāṃ jagṛhuḥ bhūruhā nṛpanandanāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ vidh class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ vidh class 6 verb]

translation: Oh Princes! A lotus-eyed girl was born to (a heavenly nymph called) Pramlocā from (a sage by name) Kaṇḍu. When the girl was abandoned (by her mother) (the presiding deity of) trees took charge of her.

devanagari: क्षुत्क्षामाया मुखे राजा सोमः पीयूषवर्षिणीम् । देशिनीं रोदमानाया निदधे स दयान्वितः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: kṣutkṣāmāyā mukhe rājā somaḥ pīyūṣavarṣiṇīm deśinīṃ rodamānāyā nidadhe sa dayānvitaḥ
translation: When she, being hungry, began to cry, king Soma (the Moon god) who was moved with compassion, put his index finger overflowing with nectar, in her mouth.

devanagari: प्रजाविसर्ग आदिष्टाः पित्रा मां अनुवर्तता । तत्र कन्यां वरारोहां तां उद्वहत मा चिरम् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: prajāvisarga ādiṣṭāḥ pitrā māṃ anuvartatā tatra kanyāṃ varārohāṃ tāṃ udvahata mā ciram
translation: You are commanded to increase population by your father who is (now) my follower. Therefore, marry that beautiful girl (with fine hips) without delay.

devanagari: अपृथग्धर्मशीलानां सर्वेषां वः सुमध्यमा । अपृथग्धर्मशीलेयं भूयात् पत्‍न्यर्पिताशया ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: apṛthagdharmaśīlānāṃ sarveṣāṃ vaḥ sumadhyamā apṛthagdharmaśīleyaṃ bhūyāt pat‍nyarpitāśayā
translation: All of you are observers of the same vows (e.g. protection of subjects) and are of similar disposition (good- naturedness, high moral character etc.). She will completely dedicate her heart to you all and hence will have the same disposition and observe the same vows. (Hence there will be no conflict). May this beautiful lady (with a slender waist) be your wife.

devanagari: दिव्यवर्षसहस्राणां सहस्रमहतौजसः । भौमान् भोक्ष्यथ भोगान् वै दिव्यान् चानुग्रहान्मम ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: divyavarṣasahasrāṇāṃ sahasramahataujasaḥ bhaumān bhokṣyatha bhogān vai divyān cānugrahānmama

translation: Without any diminution in physical vigour, you will enjoy the earthly and heavenly pleasures through my grace, for a period of one million celestial years.

devanagari: अथ मय्यनपायिन्या भक्त्या पक्वगुणाशयाः । उपयास्यथ मद्धाम निर्विद्य निरयादतः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: atha mayyanapāyinyā bhaktyā pakvaguṇāśayāḥ upayāsyatha maddhāma nirvidya nirayādataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ pac class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ pac class 4 verb]
[vocative single from √ pac class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ pac class 4 verb]

translation: Then through your firm devotion to me, the impurities (such as lust, hate) in your heart will be burnt. Being disgusted with the hell (-like pleasures of this world and the next), you will attain to my abode.

devanagari: गृहेष्वाविशतां चापि पुंसां कुशलकर्मणाम् । मद् वार्तायातयामानां न बन्धाय गृहा मताः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: gṛheṣvāviśatāṃ cāpi puṃsāṃ kuśalakarmaṇām mad vārtāyātayāmānāṃ na bandhāya gṛhā matāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ man class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative plural from √ man class 8 verb], [vocative plural from √ man class 8 verb]

[nominative plural from √ man class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ man class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative plural from √ man class 8 verb], [vocative plural from √ man class 8 verb], [accusative plural from √ man class 8 verb]

translation: Even in the case of those men who enter householder’s life, if they dedicate all their actions to me and spend their time in talking and hearing about my stories, the householder’s life does not result in a bondage to them.

devanagari: नव्यवद् हृदये यज्ज्ञो ब्रह्मैतद्‍ब्रह्मवादिभिः । न मुह्यन्ति न शोचन्ति न हृष्यन्ति यतो गताः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: navyavad hṛdaye yajjño brahmaitad‍brahmavādibhiḥ na muhyanti na śocanti na hṛṣyanti yato gatāḥ

[ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: By listening to the discourses about me, by those who are well-versed in the Vedas, I, the Omniscient Lord, enter the heart of those devotees as if I become new one (every moment). This entry (i.e. my manifestation) into the heart itself is the realization of Brahman, for those who reach me are not deluded, grieved nor are elated.

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । एवं ब्रुवाणं पुरुषार्थभाजनं । जनार्दनं प्राञ्जलयः प्रचेतसः । तद्दर्शनध्वस्ततमोरजोमला । गिरागृणन् गद्‍गदया सुहृत्तमम् ॥ २...
english_devnagari: maitreya uvāca evaṃ bruvāṇaṃ puruṣārthabhājanaṃ janārdanaṃ prāñjalayaḥ pracetasaḥ taddarśanadhvastatamorajomalā girāgṛṇan gad‍gadayā suhṛttamam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Maitreya said:
When Lord Viṣṇu (Janārdana), the conferrer of the objectives in human life (viz. dharma, artha, kāma and Mokṣa) and the best Friend of all, spoke to them thus, Pracetasas, whose impurities of tamas (ignorance) and rajas (emotions and passions) have been perfectly cleansed at his sight, bowed him with folded hands and addressed to him with their voice choked (with tears).

devanagari: प्रचेतस ऊचुः । नमो नमः क्लेशविनाशनाय । निरूपितोदारगुणाह्वयाय । मनोवचोवेगपुरोजवाय । सर्वाक्षमार्गैरगताध्वने नमः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: pracetasa ūcuḥ namo namaḥ kleśavināśanāya nirūpitodāraguṇāhvayāya manovacovegapurojavāya sarvākṣamārgairagatādhvane namaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[present middle first single], [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation:
Pracetasas said:
“Many salutations to the Lord who destroys all types of misery and whose excellent attributes and names have been considered (by the Vedas and knowers of the Brahman) as the means of attaining the highest good. Obeisance to him who surpasses the mind and speech in speed[4] and whose path is beyond the ken of all the senses.
purport:
Footnote [4]:
Cf. tad dhāvato'nyān atyeti tiṣṭhan / Īśa. 4.

devanagari: शुद्धाय शान्ताय नमः स्वनिष्ठया । मनस्यपार्थं विलसद्द्वयाय । नमो जगत्स्थानलयोदयेषु । गृहीतमायागुणविग्रहाय ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: śuddhāya śāntāya namaḥ svaniṣṭhayā manasyapārthaṃ vilasaddvayāya namo jagatsthānalayodayeṣu gṛhītamāyāguṇavigrahāya

[dative single from √ śam class 4 verb], [dative single from √ śam class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ grah class 9 verb], [accusative single from √ grah class 9 verb]

translation: In your own essential nature, you are pure, serene (and joy incarnate). The unreal appearance of duality in you is due to (the function of the) mind. For the creation, sustenance and dissolution of the universe, you have assumed forms (of Brahmā, Viṣṇu and Śiva) through the guṇas (attributes viz. Sattva, rajas and tamas) of the Māyā. Our salutations to you.

devanagari: नमो विशुद्धसत्त्वाय हरये हरिमेधसे । वासुदेवाय कृष्णाय प्रभवे सर्वसात्वताम् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: namo viśuddhasattvāya haraye harimedhase vāsudevāya kṛṣṇāya prabhave sarvasātvatām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ van class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ van class 8 verb]

translation: Obeisance to Hari who is essentially pure sattva (unmixed with rajas and tamas), and knowledge about whom terminates the saṃsāra. Bow to Vāsudeva, Kṛṣṇa. the Lord of all Sātvatas.

devanagari: नमः कमलनाभाय नमः कमलमालिने । नमः कमलपादाय नमस्ते कमलेक्षण ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: namaḥ kamalanābhāya namaḥ kamalamāline namaḥ kamalapādāya namaste kamalekṣaṇa
translation: Hail to you who have a lotus sprouted in the navel. Bow to the Lord, the wearer of a wreath of lotuses. Salutations to the God with lotus-like feet. Obeisance to you, Oh lotuseyed God!

devanagari: नमः कमलकिञ्जल्क पिशङ्गामलवाससे । सर्वभूतनिवासाय नमोऽयुङ्क्ष्महि साक्षिणे ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: namaḥ kamalakiñjalka piśaṅgāmalavāsase sarvabhūtanivāsāya namo
translation: Salutations to you who wear spotless, pure yellow garments like the filaments of the lotus. We bow to you who are the abode of all beings and a seer or witness of them all.

devanagari: रूपं भगवता त्वेतद् अशेषक्लेशसङ्क्षयम् । आविष्कृतं नः क्लिष्टानां किं अन्यद् अनुकंपितम् ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: rūpaṃ bhagavatā tvetad aśeṣakleśasaṅkṣayam āviṣkṛtaṃ naḥ kliṣṭānāṃ kiṃ anyad anukaṃpitam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [genitive plural from √ kliś class 4 verb], [genitive plural from √ kliś class 9 verb]
[genitive plural from √ kliś class 4 verb], [genitive plural from √ kliś class 9 verb]
[genitive plural from √ kliś class 4 verb], [genitive plural from √ kliś class 9 verb]

translation: You have manifested to us who are afflicted with miseries, your form which annihilates all these kinds of trouble. What more grace could be shown? (This is the highest favour shown to us).

devanagari: एतावत्त्वं हि विभुभिः भाव्यं दीनेषु वत्सलैः । यदनुस्मर्यते काले स्वबुद्ध्याभद्ररन्धन ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: etāvattvaṃ hi vibhubhiḥ bhāvyaṃ dīneṣu vatsalaiḥ yadanusmaryate kāle svabuddhyābhadrarandhana

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ bhū ], [accusative single from √ bhū ]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Oh destroyer of inauspiciousness! Only this much is to be done by the masters who are kind and compassionate to the poor that they should remember at the proper time, that the inḍigents are their own. (But in our case you have done much more than that in manifesting yourself to us).

devanagari: येनोपशान्तिर्भूतानां क्षुल्लकानामपीहताम् । अन्तर्हितोऽन्तर्हृदये कस्मान्नो वेद नाशिषः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: yenopaśāntirbhūtānāṃ kṣullakānāmapīhatām antarhito

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Such remembrance (of the poor) brings peace and happiness to beings. Abiding as you are as antaryāmin (Inner Controller) in the hearts of even the most insignificant creatures. how is it that you do not know the blessing desired by us who are your devotees?

devanagari: असौ एव वरोऽस्माकं ईप्सितो जगतः पते । प्रसन्नो भगवान्येषां अपवर्गः गुरुर्गतिः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: asau eva varo prasanno bhagavānyeṣāṃ apavargaḥ gururgatiḥ
translation: To us whom you are not only a guide to Liberation but a goal desired by us, the very fact that the Lord is pleased with us, is itself a boon sought by us.

devanagari: वरं वृणीमहेऽथापि नाथ त्वत्परतः परात् । न ह्यन्तस्त्वद् विभूतीनां सोऽनन्त इति गीयसे ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: varaṃ vṛṇīmahe na hyantastvad vibhūtīnāṃ so
translation: We, however, seek a boon from you, Oh Lord, who are beyond the cause of causes i.e. Prakṛti (the prime cause of the universe). As there is no limit to your excellences or glories, you are extolled as Ananta (Infinite).

devanagari: पारिजातेऽञ्जसा लब्धे सारङ्गोऽन्यन्न सेवते । त्वदङ्घ्रिमूलमासाद्य साक्षात्किं किं वृणीमहि ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: pārijāte tvadaṅghrimūlamāsādya sākṣātkiṃ kiṃ vṛṇīmahi

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ labh class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ labh class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ labh class 1 verb], [locative single from √ labh class 1 verb]
[nominative dual from √ labh class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ labh class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ labh class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ labh class 1 verb]

translation: When the Pārījāta tree is easily found (by a lucky chance), a bee does not resort to any other tree. (Similarly), when we have found shelter at your feet directly, what other things should we seek?[5]
purport:
Footnote [5]:
Alternatively: (i) Why should we beg for other contemptible things?
(ii) If we are to seek anything there is no end to desires—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā

devanagari: यावत्ते मायया स्पृष्टा भ्रमाम इह कर्मभिः । तावद्‍भवत् प्रसङ्गानां सङ्गः स्यान्नो भवे भवे ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: yāvatte māyayā spṛṣṭā bhramāma iha karmabhiḥ tāvad‍bhavat prasaṅgānāṃ saṅgaḥ syānno bhave bhave

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ spṛś class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ spṛś class 6 verb]
[nominative plural from √ spṛś class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ spṛś class 6 verb], [accusative plural from √ spṛś class 6 verb]

translation: So long as we, being dominated by Māyā. are wandering here in the saṃsāra due to the force of our actions (karmas), may we be blest in every life with the companionship of devotees attached to you.

devanagari: तुलयाम लवेनापि न स्वर्गं न अपुनर्भवम् । भगवत् सङ्गिसङ्गस्य मर्त्यानां किमुताशिषः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: tulayāma lavenāpi na svargaṃ na apunarbhavam bhagavat saṅgisaṅgasya martyānāṃ kimutāśiṣaḥ
translation: We do not consider (the pleasures in) heaven or even the final beatitude as equivalent to the companionship of your devotees (enjoyed even for a single moment). What of blessings like kingdom etc.) coveted by mortal beings?

devanagari: यत्रेड्यन्ते कथा मृष्टाः तृष्णायाः प्रशमो यतः । निर्वैरं यत्र भूतेषु नोद्वेगो यत्र कश्चन ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: yatreḍyante kathā mṛṣṭāḥ tṛṣṇāyāḥ praśamo yataḥ nirvairaṃ yatra bhūteṣu nodvego yatra kaścana

translation: In the company of Your votaries, sweet purifying stories (of the Lord) which pacify the thirst of desire are extolled. There is absence of enmity against all beings. Nor is there any fear to anybody.

devanagari: यत्र नारायणः साक्षाद् भगवान् न्यासिनां गतिः । संस्तूयते सत्कथासु मुक्तसङ्गैः पुनः पुनः ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: yatra nārāyaṇaḥ sākṣād bhagavān nyāsināṃ gatiḥ saṃstūyate satkathāsu muktasaṅgaiḥ punaḥ punaḥ
translation: There (in the company of devotees) the glorious Lord Nārāyaṇa Himself, the goal of Sannyāsins, is again and again extolled through sacred stories, by persons who are free from attachment.

devanagari: तेषां विचरतां पद्‍भ्यां तीर्थानां पावनेच्छया । भीतस्य किं न रोचेत तावकानां समागमः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: teṣāṃ vicaratāṃ pad‍bhyāṃ tīrthānāṃ pāvanecchayā bhītasya kiṃ na roceta tāvakānāṃ samāgamaḥ
translation: Will not the association of your devotees who with a desire to purify the sacred places, move about on foot, be liked by a person afraid (of saṃsāra)?

devanagari: वयं तु साक्षाद्‍भगवन् भवस्य । प्रियस्य सख्युः क्षणसङ्गमेन । सुदुश्चिकित्स्यस्य भवस्य मृत्योः । भिषक्तमं त्वाद्य गतिं गताः स्म ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: vayaṃ tu sākṣād‍bhagavan bhavasya priyasya sakhyuḥ kṣaṇasaṅgamena suduścikitsyasya bhavasya mṛtyoḥ bhiṣaktamaṃ tvādya gatiṃ gatāḥ sma
translation: Oh Lord! It is due to a moment’s contact with your friend, god Śiva (Bhava) that we have today attained to you direct—you who are our goal and the best physician who cures the incurable malady of (the cycle of) births and deaths.

devanagari: यन्नः स्वधीतं गुरवः प्रसादिता । विप्राश्च वृद्धाश्च सदानुवृत्त्या । आर्या नताः सुहृदो भ्रातरश्च । सर्वाणि भूतान्यनसूययैव ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: yannaḥ svadhītaṃ guravaḥ prasāditā viprāśca vṛddhāśca sadānuvṛttyā āryā natāḥ suhṛdo bhrātaraśca sarvāṇi bhūtānyanasūyayaiva

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative plural from √ vṛdh class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ vṛdh class 1 verb]

[nominative plural from √ nam class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ nam class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ nam class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ nam class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ nam class 1 verb]

translation: Oh Lord! (we seek the second boon:). Whatever we have properly studied, whatever troubles we have taken for propitiating the preceptors, Brāhmaṇas and elderly people by our compliance, the respects we paid to noble persons, friends, brothers and to all living beings without any jealousy, the austere penances we have performed without food, submerged in water for a very long period—may all this, we beseech you, contribute to the entire satisfaction and pleasure of yours—you who are the Omnipresent, the Supreme Man.

devanagari: यन्नः सुतप्तं तप एतदीश । निरन्धसां कालमदभ्रमप्सु । सर्वं तदेतत्पुरुषस्य भूम्नो । वृणीमहे ते परितोषणाय ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: yannaḥ sutaptaṃ tapa etadīśa nirandhasāṃ kālamadabhramapsu sarvaṃ tadetatpuruṣasya bhūmno vṛṇīmahe te paritoṣaṇāya

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Oh Lord! (we seek the second boon:). Whatever we have properly studied, whatever troubles we have taken for propitiating the preceptors, Brāhmaṇas and elderly people by our compliance, the respects we paid to noble persons, friends, brothers and to all living beings without any jealousy, the austere penances we have performed without food, submerged in water for a very long period—may all this, we beseech you, contribute to the entire satisfaction and pleasure of yours—you who are the Omnipresent, the Supreme Man.

devanagari: मनुः स्वयम्भूर्भगवान् भवश्च । येऽन्ये तपोज्ञानविशुद्धसत्त्वाः । अदृष्टपारा अपि यन्महिम्नः । स्तुवन्त्यथो त्वात्मसमं गृणीमः ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: manuḥ svayambhūrbhagavān bhavaśca ye adṛṣṭapārā api yanmahimnaḥ stuvantyatho tvātmasamaṃ gṛṇīmaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Manu, god Brahmā (the self-born), glorious Lord Śiva and others who have purified their hearts with penance and knowledge, praise your greatness even though they could not grasp the fullest extent of your glory. Hence we extol you according to our humble capacity.

devanagari: नमः समाय शुद्धाय पुरुषाय पराय च । वासुदेवाय सत्त्वाय तुभ्यं भगवते नमः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: namaḥ samāya śuddhāya puruṣāya parāya ca vāsudevāya sattvāya tubhyaṃ bhagavate namaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [dative single from √ śudh class 1 verb], [dative single from √ śudh class 4 verb]
[dative single from √ śudh class 1 verb], [dative single from √ śudh class 4 verb]

translation: Obeisance to you, the Supreme Man, who are equal to all and pure (unsoiled by Māyā). Salutation to you, glorious Lord Vāsudeva, who are pure sattva incarnate.”

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । इति प्रचेतोभिरभिष्टुतो हरिः । प्रीतस्तथेत्याह शरण्यवत्सलः । अनिच्छतां यानम् अतृप्तचक्षुषां । ययौ स्वधामानपवर्गवीर्यः ॥ ४...
english_devnagari: maitreya uvāca iti pracetobhirabhiṣṭuto hariḥ prītastathetyāha śaraṇyavatsalaḥ anicchatāṃ yānam atṛptacakṣuṣāṃ yayau svadhāmānapavargavīryaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ prī class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ prī class 9 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Maitreya said:
Thus extolled by Pracetasas, Hari who is affectionate to his devotees (lit. those who seek his shelter) and is of irresistible prowess, became pleased with them and conferred that boon (saying ‘So be it’.). Even though their eyes were not satiated by looking at him and they did not wish his departure, he returned to his abode.

devanagari: अथ निर्याय सलिलात् प्रचेतस उदन्वतः । वीक्ष्याकुप्यन् द्रुमैश्छन्नां गां गां रोद्धुम् इवोच्छ्रितैः ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: atha niryāya salilāt pracetasa udanvataḥ vīkṣyākupyan drumaiśchannāṃ gāṃ gāṃ roddhum ivocchritaiḥ
translation: Then the Pracetasas came out froṇi the water of the sea (-like lake). Seeing the earth (wildly) covered with trees that had grown so tall as to obstruct the way to heaven, they flared up with wrath.

devanagari: ततोऽग्निमारुतौ राजन् अमुञ्चन्मुखतो रुषा । महीं निर्वीरुधं कर्तुं संवर्तक इवात्यये ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: tato mahīṃ nirvīrudhaṃ kartuṃ saṃvartaka ivātyaye

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]
[imperfect active third single], [imperfect active third plural]
[imperfect active third single], [imperfect active third plural]

translation: For rendering the earth tree-less, they then angrily breathed out from their mouth fire and wind, just as Saṃvartaka or Kālāgni Rudra does at the time of the dissolution of the universe.

devanagari: भस्मसात् क्रियमाणान् तान् द्रुमान् वीक्ष्य पितामहः । आगतः शमयामास पुत्रान् बर्हिष्मतो नयैः ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: bhasmasāt kriyamāṇān tān drumān vīkṣya pitāmahaḥ āgataḥ śamayāmāsa putrān barhiṣmato nayaiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb]
[accusative plural from √ kṛ class 3 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 6 verb]

translation: Seeing the trees being thus reduced to ashes, god Brahmā appeared and pacified the sons of Barhiṣmat (Pracetasas) by reasoning.

devanagari: तत्रावशिष्टा ये वृक्षा भीता दुहितरं तदा । उज्जह्रुस्ते प्रचेतोभ्य उपदिष्टाः स्वयम्भुवा ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: tatrāvaśiṣṭā ye vṛkṣā bhītā duhitaraṃ tadā ujjahruste pracetobhya upadiṣṭāḥ svayambhuvā
translation: Then the surviving trees which were terrified, acted upon the advice of god Brahmā and offered their daughter to Pracetasas.

devanagari: ते च ब्रह्मण आदेशान् मारिषामुपयेमिरे । यस्यां महदवज्ञानाद् अजन्यजनयोनिजः ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: te ca brahmaṇa ādeśān māriṣāmupayemire yasyāṃ mahadavajñānād ajanyajanayonijaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect middle first single], [aorist middle first single]

translation: In compliance of the command of god Brahmā, they (Pracetasas) married Māriṣā (the daughter of the trees). Dakṣa, the former mind-born son of Brahmā (lit. one born of the unborn Viṣṇu), was born of her, on account of his (Dakṣa’s) insulting the great god Śiva (formerly).

devanagari: चाक्षुषे त्वन्तरे प्राप्ते प्राक्सर्गे कालविद्रुते । यः ससर्ज प्रजा इष्टाः स दक्षो दैवचोदितः ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: cākṣuṣe tvantare prāpte prāksarge kālavidrute yaḥ sasarja prajā iṣṭāḥ sa dakṣo daivacoditaḥ

translation: At the advent of the Cākṣuṣa Manvantara (the sixth aeon), when the previous creation was annihilated by the force of time[6] it was this Dakṣa who directed by the Lord, created the progeny as desired by him.
purport:
Footnote [6]:
When Dakṣa’s previous body was destroyed through the force of time (Bhāvāratha Dīpikā).

devanagari: यो जायमानः सर्वेषां तेजस्तेजस्विनां रुचा । स्वयोपादत्त दाक्ष्याच्च कर्मणां दक्षमब्रुवन् ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: yo jāyamānaḥ sarveṣāṃ tejastejasvināṃ rucā svayopādatta dākṣyācca karmaṇāṃ dakṣamabruvan
translation: While he was being born, he eclipsed with his own splendour the light of all luminous bodies. He came to he called Dakṣa, the competent, on account of his precision and proficiency in the performance of ritualistic acts.

devanagari: तं प्रजासर्गरक्षायां अनादिरभिषिच्य च । युयोज युयुजेऽन्यांश्च स वै सर्वप्रजापतीन् ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ prajāsargarakṣāyāṃ anādirabhiṣicya ca yuyoja yuyuje

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation:
(The beginningless) god Brahmā installed him (as a Prajāpati—the protector of the creation) and appointed him to procreate and protect the creation. Dakṣa (in his turn) employed all other Prajāpatis—progenitors of creation—to perform their respective jobs.
